summary: Manual inventory of Ralph/ralph/ralph-runner branding references for the Harness Runner rebrand. This artifact defines exact rename boundaries before metadata and runtime renames proceed.

appliesTo: TypeScript symbol prefixes and type names
output channel and status bar copy
command titles and help document titles
prompt text and evidence/task-memory fallback strings
replacementRules.notes: Prefer PascalCase Harness for symbols and title-case Harness Runner for product labels. Avoid introducing all-caps HARNESS unless a deliberate visual treatment is required later.
Keep lowercase only where the host surface expects a lowercase handle or command token.

appliesTo: extension id and activation event id
settings search queries and doc references
replacementRules.notes: Keep kebab-case for contribution points and persisted configuration keys.

preserveRules.reason: Already the canonical runtime artifact root; acceptance requires explicitly preserving it rather than renaming it again.
Reference snapshot folder is already branded for Harness and is not an implementation rename target.

Known compatibility seam noted in repo memory; downstream readers still reference the legacy key, so this must be coordinated in a later story instead of blindly renamed during broad search-and-replace.

categories.name: package-metadata-and-contributions
details: package.json still contains the old package name, display name, repository URL, activation event, command ids, chat participant id/name/fullName, configuration title, property prefixes, and icon file name.
package-lock.json mirrors the old package name and will need regeneration when package metadata changes.
The packaged VSIX artifact name still carries ralph-runner and should be treated as generated output, not hand-edited.

categories.name: runtime-entrypoints-and-command-registration
details: Entry registration still uses ralph-runner for getConfiguration, affectsConfiguration checks, registerCommand ids, status bar command ids, openSettings queries, and chat participant registration.
User-visible strings still mention RALPH Runner in the output channel, logs, startup recovery, and command guidance.
The /ralph-spec temp artifact name and request handler names are part of the future rename surface and should move with the chat participant rename, not independently.

categories.name: localization-help-and-menu-copy
details: Both language packs still expose RALPH labels in status bar text, notifications, command menu items, help titles, and participant guidance.
Help manual prose still describes the product as RALPH while correctly pointing to .harness-runner as the runtime audit trail.
progress.txt still uses the RALPH Runner heading and is a low-risk user-visible text surface to rename later.

categories.name: typescript-symbols-and-module-exports
categories.targetForm: Harness* symbol names while preserving runtime path values
details: Legacy Ralph-prefixed symbols remain in types, language-pack APIs, state manager APIs, task status types, policy config types, and workspace path helpers.
These are internal rename targets for US-003 and later, but their string values often already point at .harness-runner and must keep those runtime values intact.
workspacePaths.ts is the key rollback seam: rename exported symbol names carefully while preserving the canonical .harness-runner constant value.

categories.name: documentation-prompts-and-governance-copy
details: README still contains the largest concentration of user-facing RALPH references, including command names, chat participant examples, configuration keys, and workflow narration.
Prompt scaffolding and fallback governance text also mention RALPH and should be updated together with runtime naming so reviewer handoffs stay consistent.
Knowledge freshness warnings already mention /harness-spec in some places, so documentation updates must remove mixed branding rather than adding more aliases.

categories.name: tests-and-assertions
categories.targetForm: Match final Harness Runner naming exactly
details: Tests assert current participant ids, command ids, prompt copy, artifact paths, and fallback text. These assertions will need synchronized updates as each rename story lands.
Several newer tests already expect harness-runner reviewer-setting keys, so the suite currently reflects a mixed migration state that must be normalized deliberately.

categories.name: already-harness-surfaces
details: Runtime artifact directories, gitignore entries, several agent-map strings, and knowledge-check /harness-spec coverage already use Harness naming and should not be reverted.
These files define the current architectural truth that the rebrand stories must converge on.
